§6Coal Gasoline§f

§aCoal-derived liquid fuel with massive energy density — 6.35 MTU per bucket from coal and heavy oil!§f

Coal Gasoline is a hybrid fuel produced by processing §bHeavy Oil§f with coal products in the §bPyrolysis Oven§f or by hydrotreating §bCoal Oil§f. It has exceptional energy density at §e6.35 MTU§f and §e9.52 MHE§f per bucket, making it one of the most energy-dense liquid fuels available.

§6Production§f:
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f:
  §b–§f §e500mB§f Heavy Oil + §e1×§f Coal → §e1,000mB§f Coal Gasoline.
  §b–§f §e500mB§f Heavy Oil + Coal Powder → §e1,000mB§f.
  §b–§f §e500mB§f Heavy Oil + Petroleum Coke → §e1,000mB§f.
§b• Hydrotreater§f: §e1,000mB§f Coal Oil + §e100mB§f Liquid Hydrogen → §e800mB§f Coal Gasoline + §e150mB§f Sour Gas.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f Coal Oil → §e30mB§f Coal Gasoline + §e70mB§f Crude Oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Leaded Coal Gasoline§f (Chemical Plant / Industrial Mixer): §e10,000mB§f + Tetraethyllead → §e12,000mB§f (§e9.52 MTU§f / §e14.2 MHE§f per bucket).
§b• Generator Fuel§f: Burn in combustion generators for massive power.
§b• Chainsaw§f: Can be used as fuel.

§eTip§f: Coal Gasoline has §e10×§f the energy density of regular Gasoline! The §bPyrolysis Oven§f route using Heavy Oil is the most efficient. Lead it for even more output — the §e20%§f volume bonus from leaded mixing is well worth the Tetraethyllead cost!
